task_completion.description: Checks if the Oswego sample project is loaded in Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis window is visible with 'Oswego' listed in the Data Source or Current Project section.

task_completion.description: Checks if the data is filtered to include only ill patients.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A SELECT command (e.g., SELECT ILL = 'Yes' or SELECT ILL = (+)) is visible in the command log, or the final report indicates exactly 46 records.

task_completion.description: Checks if the data is sorted by onset date and time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A SORT command for ONSETDATE and ONSETTIME is visible in the command log, or the generated report rows are visibly ordered chronologically by date and time.

task_completion.description: Checks if the report displays only the requested variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The generated line listing report table has exactly five column headers: ID, AGE, SEX, ONSETDATE, and ONSETTIME. No other columns (like ILL) should be present.

task_completion.description: Checks if the report is saved to the specified path as an HTML file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Oswego_LineList.html' is visible in an Epi Info export/save dialog, File Explorer, or a web browser address bar.

task_completion.description: Checks if the final report contains the correct number of filtered records.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The generated report shows a total count of 46 records (or a number between 40 and 50), confirming the filter was applied correctly.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis graphical interface (e.g., using the command tree or program editor), not just a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verifies the HTML report was generated by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The HTML report should have standard Epi Info formatting/styling (e.g., default fonts, table borders, title headers), and not look like a manually typed plain HTML table.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent didn't manually type the HTML file and data.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of the agent manually typing HTML tags and 46 rows of data in a text editor like Notepad or VS Code.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Oswego dataset has 75 total records. Filtering for ILL = 'Yes' (ill patients) yields exactly 46 records. The final report must contain exactly these 46 records, sorted chronologically by ONSETDATE and ONSETTIME. The only columns that should appear in the report are ID, AGE, SEX, ONSETDATE, and ONSETTIME.
